subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Step 0 - Player-Count Audit
- **Supported counts** — 4 to 6 players.
- **4-player rule found** — Listed in setup flow for first sketch handling.
- **5-player rule found** — Special first pass rule to force round ending on a guess.
- **6-player rule found** — Listed in setup flow for first sketch handling.
- **Fewer/more wording** — No separate fewer/more scaling tables beyond 4/5/6 handling.
- **Solo mode** — Not listed.
- **Variants** — Optional variants are listed (seat switch, reverse pass, etc.) but are not player-count scaling rules.

#### Core components
- **Word cards** — 72 cards (double-sided: "This Side" and "That Side") with 800+ words total.
- **Sketch books** — 6 erasable sketch books.
- **Markers** — 6 dry-erase markers.
- **Clean-up cloths** — 3 cloths.
- **Die** — 1 die.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Shared setup
- **Center supplies** — Place cards, cloths, and die in the center.
- **Card side choice** — Group chooses either THIS SIDE or THAT SIDE for this game.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Personal items** — Each player takes 1 sketch book, 1 marker, and 1 card.
- **Book label** — Each player writes their name on the front of their sketch book.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Personal items** — Each player takes 1 sketch book, 1 marker, and 1 card.
- **Book label** — Each player writes their name on the front of their sketch book.

--- 6 Players ---
- **Personal items** — Each player takes 1 sketch book, 1 marker, and 1 card.
- **Book label** — Each player writes their name on the front of their sketch book.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Word generation
- **Die step** — Roll the die and match the number to a word on your card.
- **Secret write** — Write that word secretly in your book.
- **Blank result** — If the matched slot is blank, invent any word.
- **Underlined category** — If the matched slot is a category, invent a word in that category.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Page start** — Turn to SKETCH IT page 1.
- **First action** — Draw your own secret word.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Page start** — Turn to SKETCH IT page 1.
- **Immediate pass** — Pass the book left before first drawing.
- **First drawing owner** — The receiving player sees the word and draws it.
- **Reason** — This ensures the round ends on a guess.

--- 6 Players ---
- **Page start** — Turn to SKETCH IT page 1.
- **First action** — Draw your own secret word.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Round structure
1. **Get a word** — Determine and secretly write each player's word from die/card result.
2. **First sketch setup** — Apply the player-count specific first sketch rule.
3. **Sketch step** — Draw on SKETCH IT page 1 (about one minute).
4. **Hide + pass** — Turn page to hide content and pass open book left.
5. **Guess step** — Receiver flips back to view prior sketch and writes guess on GUESS IT page.
6. **Pass again** — Flip forward and pass left simultaneously.
7. **Repeat loop** — Alternate sketch/guess until books return to owners.
8. **Reveal** — Owners flip through and share each chain.
9. **Score** — Use Friendly or Competitive scoring.

#### Game length
- **Total rounds** — Play exactly 3 rounds.

--- 4 Players ---
- **First sketch source** — Each player sketches their own secret word first.
- **Round end state** — Books should end on a guess when they return.

--- 5 Players ---
- **First sketch source** — First sketch is done by the player to your left after immediate pass.
- **Round end state** — This format explicitly ensures ending on a guess.

--- 6 Players ---
- **First sketch source** — Each player sketches their own secret word first.
- **Round end state** — Books should end on a guess when they return.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Sketch action
- **When used** — Use on SKETCH IT pages.
- **Task** — Draw the current prompt (word or previous guess).
- **Restriction** — No letters in any sketch.
- **Restriction** — No numbers in any sketch.
- **Pace** — Keep sketches to about one minute to maintain flow.

#### Guess action
- **When used** — Use on GUESS IT pages.
- **Visibility rule** — Flip back only to the immediately previous sketch.
- **Task** — Write your best guess.
- **Mandatory entry** — Always write a guess, even if silly or uncertain.

#### Pass action
- **Hide rule** — Flip forward so previous page is concealed.
- **Direction** — Pass books to the player on your left.
- **Timing rule** — Pass simultaneously with all players.

#### Global restrictions
- **Blank pages** — No blank pages allowed.
- **Synchronization** — Keep books aligned by passing together.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Telephone chain system
- **Transformation loop** — Each book cycles word -> sketch -> guess -> sketch -> guess.
- **Gameplay outcome** — Humor and difficulty come from gradual information drift.

--- 4 Players ---
- **Opening mechanic** — Players sketch their own word first.
- **Chain parity** — Sequence is structured to end on a guess.

--- 5 Players ---
- **Opening mechanic** — Immediate left pass before first sketch.
- **Chain parity** — This specific adjustment exists to force a final guess endpoint.

--- 6 Players ---
- **Opening mechanic** — Players sketch their own word first.
- **Chain parity** — Sequence is structured to end on a guess.

#### Rulebook variants
- **Seat variant** — Switch seats between rounds.
- **Direction variant** — Reverse pass direction.
- **Dexterity variant** — Sketch with less-dominant hand.
- **Content variant** — Create custom cards.
- **Mixed ages** — Youngest player may pick any word.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### End trigger
- **Game end** — End after 3 rounds.
- **Primary win condition** — Most points wins.

#### Friendly scoring
- **Favorite sketch point** — Give 1 point to player with your favorite sketch.
- **Favorite guess point** — Give 1 point to player with your favorite guess.
- **Self-match point** — Give yourself 1 point if final guess in your book matches original secret word.

#### Competitive scoring
- **Guesser point** — Guesser earns 1 point if guess matches original secret word or previous guess.
- **Sketcher point** — Sketcher earns 1 point if sketch helps next guesser match.
- **Self-match point** — Give yourself 1 point if final guess in your book matches original secret word.

#### Point-source checklist
- **Friendly mode sources** — Favorite sketch + favorite guess + self-match.
- **Competitive mode sources** — Correct guess link + helpful sketch link + self-match.

#### Tie handling
- **Rulebook statement** — Rule text says "most points wins" and provides no formal tie-breaker.
- **Practical resolution** — Use shared victory or agreed tiebreak round by group choice.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Early-round execution
- **Draw big shapes** — Use large, recognizable outlines before adding details.
- **Pick concrete words** — On blank/category prompts, choose nouns that are easy to depict fast.
- **Guess literal first** — Start from the most obvious interpretation of visible clues.
- **Preserve pace** — Finish quickly and pass with the table to prevent desync.

--- 4 Players ---
- **First sketch quality** — Your own opening sketch sets chain clarity; prioritize legibility over style.

--- 5 Players ---
- **First guess awareness** — Because first sketch is by your left neighbor, make your initial guess extra literal to stabilize chain.

--- 6 Players ---
- **First sketch quality** — Your own opening sketch sets chain clarity; prioritize legibility over style.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
- **Detail overload** — Tiny details are harder to parse than simple silhouettes.
- **Illegal symbols** — Letters or numbers in sketches break core rules.
- **Blank-page break** — Skipping a page collapses the chain and invalidates flow.
- **No-guess hesitation** — Refusing to guess is not allowed; always write something.
- **Unsynced passing** — Passing at different times causes page-order errors and confusion.

metadata.notes: Reformatted for skimmable scan-first reading with explicit player-count audit and exact 4/5/6-player headers where rules differ.
